site.name: Codka Bukaanka
site.description: Codka Bukaanka (codkabukaanka.com) is a verified patient voice and healthcare platform documenting medication errors, misdiagnosis, and patient safety incidents in Somalia.

home.heroSubtitle: The Patient Registry is an independent database established to collect and record confirmed cases that affect patients and healthcare services. Every report undergoes careful investigation and verification before it is included in the registry.
home.missionText: To preserve verified records of healthcare incidents, support transparency, and enable searchable public access to documented patient safety cases.
home.searchPlaceholder: Type a name, hospital, medicine, or city…
home.searchHint: Type at least 2 letters, then press Search or pick a suggestion.
home.guideIntro: You do not need computer experience. Follow these simple steps.
home.step1Text: Enter a hospital name, patient name, doctor, medicine, city, or any word from a case.
home.step2Text: Tap the green Search button or choose a name from the list that appears.
home.step3Text: Click a card to read the full verified report.
home.browseSubtitle: Tap a topic below to see lists you can scroll.
home.browseSearchDesc: Look up any name or word in the archive
home.categoriesSubtitle: Tap a topic to see related cases.

search.title: Search the Archive
search.description: Find hospitals, patients, doctors, medicines, and verified case reports.
search.placeholder: Type a name, hospital, medicine, or city…
search.hint: Type at least 2 letters. Suggestions appear as you type.
search.startText: Use the box above. You can search by hospital name, patient name, doctor, medicine, or words from a report.
search.startTip1: You do not need an account.
search.startTip2: Optional filters narrow results by hospital or date.
search.submitCaseTitle: Need to report a new incident?
search.submitCaseText: The archive only lists verified cases. To report something new, fill out the secure submission form — our team reviews every report before anything is published.
search.submitCaseSuggest: Submit a new case for review
search.filtersHelp: Only use these if you already know the hospital or dates. Most people only need the search box.
search.noResults: No cases match your search criteria.

report.disclaimer: This report is part of a public documentation archive. Information is compiled from evidence reviewed by administrators. Publication does not constitute a legal determination of liability or wrongdoing.

about.title: About Us
about.description: Learn about Codka Bukaanka (codkabukaanka.com) — a patient voice and healthcare platform preserving verified patient safety records in Somalia.
about.whatText: CodkaBukaanka (Diiwaanka Bukaanka) is an independent public documentation archive established to collect, verify, and preserve records of healthcare incidents that affect patients in Somalia. We document medication errors, misdiagnosis, treatment failures, and other patient safety events so that families, journalists, researchers, and the public can access verified information in one searchable place.
about.missionText: To preserve verified records of healthcare incidents, support transparency and accountability, and enable searchable public access to documented patient safety cases — without turning medical harm into social media content.
about.principlesSubtitle: Three principles guide how records are verified, searched, and published.
about.principle1Text: Every published case goes through a structured review. Reports appear only after evidence is examined and the record meets our verification standards.
about.principle2Text: Anyone can search hospitals, patients, doctors, medications, and case reports without creating an account.
about.principle3Text: Records follow one documentation process and are published only after review. Tips and correction requests are welcome, but nothing goes live until evidence and details meet our standards — keeping the archive consistent and trustworthy.
about.documentsIntro: The archive focuses on confirmed or well-evidenced incidents involving patient safety, including:
about.documentItem1: Medication errors — wrong drug, wrong dose, or dangerous interactions
about.documentItem3: Surgical or procedural complications linked to care failures
about.documentItem4: Hospital and clinic incidents affecting named patients
about.documentItem5: Patterns across facilities, providers, and medicines when supported by evidence
about.processIntro: Cases follow a structured workflow before they appear in the public archive:
about.processStep1: Incident details are compiled from submitted information and available evidence.
about.processStep2: The record is reviewed internally and assigned a verification status.
about.processStep3: Supporting evidence (documents, images, timelines) is attached where appropriate.
about.processStep4: Only published cases appear in search results and public listings.
about.notItem1: This archive is not medical advice. Do not use it to make treatment decisions.
about.notItem2: Publication does not constitute a legal finding of liability, negligence, or criminal wrongdoing.
about.notItem3: We are an independent documentation project, not a government regulator or court.
about.notItem4: If you believe a record contains an error, use the correction request form — we review all submissions.
about.submitCaseText: Report a healthcare incident for our team to review. We verify every submission before anything is published.
about.contactSectionText: Reach out for general questions, media requests, or to request a factual correction to a published record.
about.otherContactHint: For general questions or factual corrections:

caseSubmission.weeklyLimitNote: For security, each connection can submit one case per week. Submissions are reviewed by our team — nothing is published automatically.
caseSubmission.title: Short title for this case
caseSubmission.reasonForVisit: Why did the patient visit the hospital or clinic?
caseSubmission.incidentDescription: Describe what went wrong in detail
caseSubmission.evidenceHelp: Upload photos, videos, or documents (PDF, Word). You can add many files. Also describe anything that is not in the files.
caseSubmission.evidenceNotes: Evidence notes (optional if you uploaded files)
caseSubmission.evidenceUploadHelp: Add hospital records, prescriptions, photos, videos, or other documents. Each file can be up to 50MB.

caseSubmission.evidenceRequired: Upload at least one file or write evidence notes (10+ characters).

legal.submitCaseDescription: Use this form to report a healthcare incident for our team to review. Fill in as much detail as you can. We verify every submission before anything is published.
